title: fix: capture end_time at stream completion for streamed responses
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Moves the tracking `endTime` capture from right-after-`next()` (upstream header arrival) to after `trackResponse()` — which already consumes the SSE tee to completion — so `end_time`/`response_time` cover the whole request for streamed text instead of time-to-first-byte
- Adds a middleware test streaming SSE chunks with real delays, asserting `responseTime` covers the full stream duration
- Enables tokens/sec = `completion_tokens / response_time` in `model_health.pipe` with **no `generation_event` schema change** (simplifies #12287)

## Evidence the old value was wrong
Prod data: completions of 65,535 tokens logged with `response_time` of 1.1–2.4s (implied 27k–57k tok/s, physically impossible); 39% of >1500-token completions implied >400 tok/s.

## Intended semantic shift (accepted)
`latency_p50/p95`, `avg_response_ms` (model_health, public_model_stats, weekly_health_stats) and the activity dashboard shift from TTFB to full duration for streamed text — expect a one-time step up for stream-heavy models at deploy. Adversarial review swept the monorepo: no alerting, billing, abuse, or dedup logic keys on these values; realtime billing has its own timing and is unaffected. Binary bodies (image/audio) are not read by tracking and keep header-arrival semantics.

## Test plan
- [x] `tracking-observability.test.ts` (14 tests) + `error-observability` + `billing-deduction` + `usage-headers` all pass
- [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean, biome clean

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Stopwatch fixed
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where our internal stopwatch was stopping at the first byte of a streamed response instead of the last. Now our tokens-per-second metrics are actually bound by the laws of physics.
gist.summary: Updated the tracking middleware to capture `end_time` at stream completion rather than time-to-first-byte, fixing artificially inflated tokens-per-second metrics for streamed text.
gist.impact: Internal dashboards and model health metrics will now reflect the full generation duration for streamed responses instead of just the initial connection time.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding a stopwatch, timing a stream of data flowing from a retro computer, with the Nomnom creature eating the data stream.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot holding a tiny stopwatch, timing a glowing stream of text flowing from a retro computer monitor. The round tan Nomnom creature happily eats the end of the data stream. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
